title: GKE CI/CD Pipeline
aim: To build a complete CI/CD pipeline using Google Kubernetes Engine, Artifact Registry, and Cloud Build.

steps.description: Enable GKE, Cloud Build, and Artifact Registry APIs.
Create an Artifact Registry repository for Docker images.
Create a GKE cluster to host your application.
Prepare a 'cloudbuild.yaml' file that defines build and push steps.
Manually submit a build to verify the configuration.
Create a Cloud Build Trigger linked to your GitHub repository.
Add a CD step to your pipeline that applies the manifest to the GKE cluster.
Push a code change to GitHub and verify that the pipeline automatically builds and deploys the new version.

commands.purpose: Deploys resources to a Kubernetes cluster

viva.question: What is Cloud Build?
viva.answer: A serverless CI/CD platform that executes your builds on Google Cloud infrastructure.

viva.question: What is Artifact Registry?
viva.answer: A managed service for storing, managing, and securing your build artifacts (like Docker images).

result: Successfully automated the build and deployment process for a containerized application.
conclusion: Learned the power of DevSecOps by automating the full software delivery lifecycle.
